summary: Misdemeanor to lodge in any building, structure, vehicle, or place, public or private, without permission of the owner or person entitled to possession
Trespass provisions frequently used against encampments on private or restricted public land
Public nuisance statutes sometimes invoked for encampment abatement
Prohibits sitting, lying, sleeping, or storing property in public right-of-way within designated distances of sensitive uses (schools, shelters, parks) after council resolution

ordinance_name: Storage of personal property in public areas
summary: Limits storage of personal property in public areas; authorizes removal and disposal of bulky items; core legal authority for LA sweeps
Prohibits sitting or lying on public sidewalks 7am–11pm citywide
Prohibits remaining in parks overnight / erecting structures; used with Police Code tent provisions post-Grants Pass
Prohibits camping and storage of camp paraphernalia on public or private property without permit; expanded enforcement under Measure O (2022) Emergency Shelter and Enforcement Act
Prohibits camping/overnight presence in city parks
Bans encampments on public property when shelter available; bans at all times near schools, shelters, parks, transit hubs, waterways regardless of shelter availability
Citywide camping ban adopted post-Grants Pass (2024); prohibits camping on public property citywide
Park closure and encampment abatement provisions; expanded RV/oversized-vehicle restrictions
Council-adopted administrative policy (2020) governing closures/sweeps rather than criminal ordinance; enforcement via PC 647(e)/nuisance
Sidewalk obstruction ordinance and encampment policy enforcement
